cards.front: What is the most common surgical emergency?
cards.back: Acute appendicitis

cards.front: What is the lifetime risk of developing appendicitis?
What is the most common position of the appendix?
cards.back: Retrocaecal (65%)

cards.front: What is the blood supply of the appendix?
cards.back: Appendicular artery (branch of ileocolic artery, which is a branch of superior mesenteric artery)

cards.front: Where is McBurney's point located?
cards.back: Junction of lateral 1/3 and medial 2/3 of the line joining the anterior superior iliac spine (ASIS) to the umbilicus

cards.front: What is pathognomonic for acute appendicitis?
cards.back: Migration of pain from periumbilical region to right iliac fossa

cards.front: What is Rovsing's sign?
cards.back: Pain in the right iliac fossa when pressure is applied to the left iliac fossa (indicates peritoneal irritation)

cards.front: What is the Psoas sign and what does it indicate?
cards.back: Pain on extension of the right hip. Indicates retrocaecal appendicitis (appendix lying on psoas muscle)

cards.front: What is the Obturator sign?
cards.back: Pain on internal rotation of the flexed right hip. Indicates pelvic appendicitis

cards.text: The ALVARADO score uses the mnemonic {{MANTRELS}} and has a maximum score of {{10}}

cards.front: What does MANTRELS stand for in Alvarado score?
cards.back:
M - Migration of pain
A - Anorexia
N - Nausea/Vomiting
T - Tenderness in RIF (2 points)
R - Rebound tenderness
E - Elevated temperature
L - Leukocytosis (2 points)
S - Shift to left

cards.front: What Alvarado score indicates high probability of appendicitis requiring surgery?
cards.back: Score ≥7

cards.front: What is the most common complication of acute appendicitis?
cards.back: Perforation (20-30%)

cards.front: What is an appendicular mass?
cards.back: Inflamed appendix walled off by omentum and adjacent loops of bowel, forming a palpable mass in RIF. Usually forms after 48-72 hours of onset

cards.front: What is the Ochsner-Sherren regimen?
cards.back: Conservative management of appendicular mass: NPO, IV fluids, antibiotics, monitoring. If mass resolves, interval appendicectomy at 6-8 weeks

cards.front: When is immediate surgery indicated in appendicular mass?
cards.back: Signs of peritonitis, mass increasing in size, sepsis, failure of conservative management

cards.front: What is the gold standard investigation for appendicitis?
cards.back: CT scan of abdomen (sensitivity >95%, specificity >95%)

cards.front: What is the preferred surgical approach for appendicitis?
cards.back: Laparoscopic appendicectomy (less wound infection, faster recovery, better cosmesis)

cards.front: What antibiotic regimen is used for complicated appendicitis?
cards.back: Ceftriaxone + Metronidazole for 5-7 days (covers gram-negative and anaerobes)

cards.front: What is the most common surgical emergency in pregnancy?
cards.back: Acute appendicitis

cards.front: What investigation is preferred for suspected appendicitis in pregnancy?
cards.back: USG first, MRI if inconclusive (avoid CT due to radiation)

cards.front: Why does pain migrate from periumbilical to RIF in appendicitis?
cards.back: Initial visceral pain (from appendix stretching) is referred to T10 dermatome (periumbilical). When inflammation reaches parietal peritoneum, localized somatic pain occurs in RIF

cards.front: What is the most common cause of appendiceal obstruction?
cards.back: Faecolith (fecalite) - hardened fecal matter

cards.front: What is pylephlebitis?
cards.back: Suppurative thrombophlebitis of the portal vein - a rare but serious complication of appendicitis

cards.front: What is the incision used for open appendicectomy?
cards.back: Gridiron (McBurney's) incision - oblique incision at McBurney's point, muscle-splitting approach
